data.title: Anthropic settles to pay $1.5B to authors in landmark copyright case over AI training
data.description:
Anthropic has settled a civil suit and agreed to pay authors $1.5B in a landmark copyright case involving the use of training artificial intelligence models on pirated copies of books.

Anthropic, which is backed by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and other tech giants, was accused by several authors of downloading pirated copies of books to train its Claude chatbot. According to details of the settlement, Anthropic will pay $3,000 for each of the approximately 500,000 pirated books used to train Claude. It will also destroy two of its datasets. 

The $1.5B settlement deal must still be approved by U.S. District Judge William Alsup for the Northern District of California in San Francisco. A hearing for the settlement has been set for Monday, Sept. 8. 

"The proposed settlement well surpasses other copyright recoveries, will provide meaningful compensation for each Class Work, and will set a precedent of AI companies paying for their use of pirated websites like Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror," reads the motion for the settlement, which was filed today.

The case, which is filed under Bartz et al v. Anthropic PBC, 24-cv-05417, began last year when authors Andrea Bartz, Charles Graeber and Kirk Wallace Johnson sued the startup. A larger group of writers and publishers eventually joined the suit. 

Alsup issued a split decision in his summary judgment order earlier this summer. He determined that training AI models on purchased, copyrighted books that were digitized was fair use. However, training models on copyrighted books obtained through pirate websites is not.

"The Court reached different conclusions regarding different sources of training data," according to court documents. "It found that reproducing purchased and scanned books to train AI constituted fair use. However, the Court denied summary judgment on the copyright infringement claims related to the works Anthropic obtained from Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror."

"The proposed settlement well surpasses other copyright recoveries, will provide meaningful compensation for each Class Work, and will set a precedent of AI companies paying for their use of pirated websites like Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror," according to the settlement. 

The settlement sends a "strong message to the AI industry that there are serious consequences when they pirate authors' works to train their AI, robbing those least able to afford it," said Authors Guild CEO Mary Rasenberger.

If Anthropic had not agreed to a settlement, the case was expected to go to trial this December, which could have eventually cost Anthropic even more. Anthropic faces another civil suit by Reddit (RDDT) for allegedly using the social media company's data without a licensing agreement.

Other AI companies are facing similar suits. OpenAI and its primary backer, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), have been sued by the New York Times (NYT) for alleged copyright infringement for using the newspaper's content to train AI models.

In June, a federal judge sided with Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) in dismissing a copyright infringement lawsuit of authors who alleged the company stole their works to train its AI models.

data.title: DBV Technologies launches $150M ATM offering
data.description:
• DBV Technologies (NASDAQ:DBVT) filed a prospectus supplement with the SEC for an at-the-market offering up to $150M of American Depositary Shares.
  • Each ADS represents five ordinary shares of the company.
  • The new ATM Program replaces the prior ATM program from May 2022, with similar terms and conditions.
  • Proceeds will primarily fund the Biologics License Application and potential launch of the VIASKIN Peanut patch for toddlers aged 1-3 years.
  • Additional funds will support ongoing development of the VIASKIN technology platform, working capital, and general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Pintec announces private placement of class A ordinary shares
data.description:
• Pintec Technology (NASDAQ:PT) has entered into a share transfer agreement with ZIITECH and certain of its shareholders for the transfer of 715,521 ordinary shares of ZIITECH to the company, in exchange for an aggregate of 83.72M Class A ordinary shares of the company.
  • PT -5.26% after hours to $0.9.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Clene files to sell 491,496 shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Clene (NASDAQ:CLNN) filed to sell 491,496 million shares of common stock.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of common stock by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Steakholder Foods announces one-for-eight reverse ADS split
data.description:
• Steakholder Foods (NASDAQ:STKH) will change its ADS ratio from 1 ADS representing 500 ordinary shares to 1 ADS representing 4,000 ordinary shares, effective September 10, 2025.
  • The adjustment acts as a one-for-eight reverse ADS split for ADS holders, with no action required from investors.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Walmart CFO enters stock trading plan for 40K shares
data.description:
• Walmart (NYSE:WMT) CFO John Rainey has entered into a stock trading plan to sell 20K shares on February 2, 2026, and on March 2, 2026 as part of his long-term asset diversification, tax, and financial planning strategy.
  • The notice was filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission after the market close on Friday.
  • The sales are also in accordance with Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) Insider Trading Policy and will be conducted at prevailing market prices. Rainey will have no discretion or control over the timing or effectuation of any transactions in company securities.
  • Under Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) stock ownership guidelines, Rainey is required to hold company stock equal in value to at least five times his base salary. Following the two transactions, Rainey will continue to satisfy the requirements of this condition.
  • After closing lower for the first time in six days, Walmart (WMT) shares continued to lose ground in after-hours trading.

data.title: Adamas Trust files $500M mixed securities shelf

  • Filing.

data.title: Southern's Georgia Power wins approval for five new solar facilities
data.description:
Southern Co. (NYSE:SO) said Friday its Georgia Power subsidiary received approval from the Georgia Public Service Commission to certify five new utility-scale solar site power purchase agreements.

The new solar facilities with a combined 1,068 MW of solar capacity will be built and maintained by third-party companies that successfully bid projects in the CARES 2023 Request for Proposal, the company said.

The five sites will be built in the following locations: Mitchell County, 25-year PPA with 260 MW capacity; Laurens County, 20-year-PPA with 225 MW capacity Coffee County, 30-year PPA with 200 MW; Jefferson County, 20-year-PPA with 200 MW; and Wilkinson County, 20-year-PPA with 183 MW.

Georgia Power's 2025 Integrated Resource Plan, approved in July, highlights the additional procurement of a total of up to 4,000 MW of renewable resources by 2035; Southern (NYSE:SO) said it expects to issue RFPs in 2026 for these new resources, which would expand the company's renewable resource portfolio to ~11K MW by 2035.
